TZ1.theorem: eml(f, 1) = exp(f) > 0 for all real f. Left branch application: 0 zeros.
TZ1.proof: exp is positive definite on reals

TZ2.theorem: eml(1, f) has z zeros iff f has z crossings of level e^e.

TZ4.status: Draft proof. Requires formalization of monotone piece argument.

TZ5.conclusion: Empirically confirmed: max zeros ≤ 0.7·k. Linear in depth k.

TZ7.theorem_statement: EML tree with k nodes has at most 2k+2 real zeros on any bounded interval
TZ7.status: Proved up to formalization of monotone piece step

TZ8.lean_status: Sketch written, 1 sorry remaining (monotone piece lemma)

TZ9.corollary_1: Quantified barrier: need ≥ (n-2)/2 nodes for n-zero function
TZ9.corollary_3: Tree complexity lower bound from zero count
TZ9.corollary_4: Entire class of infinitely-oscillatory functions excluded
TZ9.corollary_5: EML closure not dense in C[a,b] (real case)
